title: Modder successfully runs Counter-Strike clone at 60 FPS on the original Sony PSP created his own Rust-based 3D engine to power 480 x 272 gameplay, also works on PS Vita
summary: A modder developed a 60 FPS Counter-Strike clone for the PSP, utilizing a custom Rust-based 3D engine. The game runs at 480 x 272 resolution. It also works on the PS Vita.
insight: PSP hardware capabilities can surprise with modern game engine performance

title: Researchers create programmable material that can steer heat and remember its state without power breakthrough could eventually aid AI chip cooling and silicon photonics
summary: Researchers created a programmable thermal material that steers heat without power, benefiting AI chips and other technologies.
insight: Breakthrough could revolutionize heat management in high-performance computing devices.

title: Boutique DIY Hi-Fi solution lets you repurpose your old IDE optical drives as a standalone audio player $190 CD-ROM Player 01 features a laser-cut enclosure and a custom PCB
summary: The $190 CD-ROM Player 01 is a unique DIY audio solution that repurposes old IDE optical drives into standalone audio players, featuring a stylish laser-cut enclosure and custom PCB.
insight: Reduces electronic waste through creative repurposing of outdated devices.

title: Tesla's AI5 with 2nm-class node tapes out at Samsung Foundry production starts soon, months after TSMC tape out
summary: Tesla's AI5 processor with 2nm-class node has taped out at Samsung Foundry. This marks Samsung's entry into production for the AI5 processor, following TSMC's tape out. Production is set to begin soon.
insight: Samsung joins TSMC in AI5 production, expanding 2nm-class node opportunities

title: Upcoming MSI Afterburner update adds heatmap to V/F curve editor to show your GPU's boosting behavior new feature shoots for better overclocks with more data
summary: A new heatmap in MSI Afterburner's V/F curve editor displays boosting behavior, helping with overclock optimization.
insight: Better overclocks through real-world GPU boosting behavior visualization available.

title: Micron commits $500 million to GlobalWafers' Texas wafer plant as it raises U.S. spending to $250 billion memory maker aims to manufacture 40% of DRAM in the US by 2035
summary: Micron commits $500M to GlobalWafers' Texas plant, part of $250B US spending goal. The investment aims to manufacture 40% of DRAM in the US by 2035.
insight: Reducing reliance on foreign suppliers for critical semiconductor components.

title: Intel's new space-grade Starfire chip is a Panther Lake SoC that puts an 18A CPU into orbit chip designed for the US government leverages Intel 3 for the GPU
summary: Intel's Starfire chip is a space-grade SoC for the U.S. government, featuring an 18A CPU and leveraging Intel 3 for the GPU.
insight: Advancing space exploration with high-performance U.S. government-grade technology developed.

title: Cooler Master HAF II 500 Case Review: New HAF delivers on its name, with impressive airflow and a roomy chassis
summary: The Cooler Master HAF II 500 offers excellent airflow and solid thermal performance, making it an attractive option for PC builders seeking a roomy and quiet case. Its massive 220mm fans provide efficient cooling, and the spacious interior allows for flexible building options. The case is priced competitively at around $200.
insight: High airflow efficiency at an affordable price range matters.

title: Meta expands colossal Hyperion AI supercluster plans to 5GW, pushes Louisiana investment past $50 billion as AI race accelerates says it plans to invest over $1 billion in local infrastructure improvements
summary: Meta expands Hyperion AI supercluster to 5GW, lifting Louisiana investment to $50 billion. This significant expansion aims to boost AI computing capacity. The company plans to invest $1 billion in local infrastructure improvements.
insight: Rapidly increasing AI computing capacity fuels Meta's competitiveness and growth.

title: AMD FSR Multi-Frame Generation with 8x mode spotted experimental driver settings could hint at FSR's next evolution
summary: Experimental driver settings hint at next-gen FSR technologies from AMD. The discovered options suggest a potential 8x Multi-Frame Generation mode. However, there's no confirmation on its current form.
insight: Potential for improved FSR performance with next-generation technologies

title: Flashforge Creator 5 review: Basic and affordable tool changer
summary: The Flashforge Creator 5 tool changer is a basic yet effective solution for budget-conscious 3D printing enthusiasts. It offers a nearly perfect balance of affordability and functionality. This device makes 3D printing more accessible to newcomers and hobbyists.
insight: Budget-friendly tool changer for accessible 3D printing solutions.

title: Apple's rumored M7 Ultra targets 1.5TB of memory and Blackwell-class AI performance, report claims monster 2028 offering would depend on memory shortage easing
summary: Apple's M7 Ultra chip targets up to 1.5 TB of unified memory and Blackwell-class AI performance.
insight: Breakthrough AI capabilities could revolutionize high-end computing and industries like healthcare.

title: Meet Moddo Pinch The world s smallest 32-bit Arduino-compatible board (2026 Edition)
summary: Moddo's Pinch is a 10.9 x 10.5 mm 32-bit Arduino-compatible board with a Microchip SAMD11 MCU and features like USB-C, RGB LED, and 12 GPIOs.
insight: Miniaturization enables compact, low-power IoT applications and wearables.

title: IoTLabs wM-Bus gateway An ESP32 device with Wireless M-Bus connectivity for Home Assistant
summary: IoTLabs wM-Bus gateway is an ESP32 device that integrates Wireless M-Bus connectivity for seamless data reception from smart meters and sensors, designed for Home Assistant integration. It features a 1.3-inch OLED display for live measurements and supports T1 and C1 frames. The device includes Wi-Fi, BLE, and 868 MHz radio receiver capabilities.
insight: Wireless M-Bus connectivity enables smart home energy monitoring and automation

title: Ambient Scientific GPX10 Pro MCU delivers years of always-on AI on a coin-cell battery
summary: The Ambient Scientific GPX10 Pro MCU offers a low-power solution for AI applications, enabling years of always-on operation on a coin-cell battery. It features ten programmable AI cores, ultra-low power ADC, and an Arm Cortex-M4F core for non-AI workloads. This SoC is suitable for power-constrained edge devices.
insight: Low-power AI processing extends battery life in edge devices.

title: Best of Sensors: Sensirion Redefines Smart Infrastructure with CO2 Sensing
summary: Sensirion's SCD53 carbon dioxide sensor won a 'Best of Sensors Award' in the Smart Infrastructure Category. The award was accepted at Sensors Converge on May 6 in Santa Clara, Calif. This achievement highlights the sensor's impact in smart infrastructure development.
insight: Accurate CO2 sensing enhances smart infrastructure efficiency and sustainability.

title: Delicate touch: Tactile sensors help humanoids get a grip
summary: Bosch is witnessing significant growth in MEMS technology with humanoids, driven by tactile sensors that help robots better interact with their environment. This is accompanied by an increase in investment for industry robots, with some startups seeing their investment double. The technology has the potential to enhance the capabilities of humanoids.
insight: Tactile sensors improve humanoids' ability to interact with physical objects.

title: Dell Alienware AW3426DW Review: The New Ultrawide OLED to Beat
summary: Dell's Alienware AW3426DW is a refined ultrawide OLED monitor with improved features and lower price.
insight: Innovative QD-OLED panel offers brighter HDR and improved text clarity.

title: Apple's iOS 27 public beta arrives with Siri AI, faster app launches, and more
summary: Apple releases iOS 27 public beta with AI-enhanced Siri and faster app launches, but some features are limited to newer models due to RAM requirements.
insight: New iOS limits AI features to latest iPhone and iPad models.

title: Zimbra patched a flaw that let hackers hijack accounts just by sending an email
summary: Zimbra has released a patch to fix a stored XSS vulnerability, which could be exploited through the Classic Web Client. Users are advised to update to version 10.1.19 as soon as possible to maintain account security.
insight: Vulnerability lets hackers hijack accounts via malicious email

title: This GE Appliances plant couldn't keep workers until it built an app, like an Uber for factory work
summary: The employees log in to an app and choose when to work, signing up for four-hour blocks that fit their schedules. In a typical week, roughly half the pool around 450 workers pick up shifts, averaging about 24 hours. Read Entire Article

title: Firefox is switching to a faster release schedule, just like Chrome and Edge
summary: Mozilla's Firefox will adopt a faster release schedule, starting with Firefox 155 in September.
insight: Competition with Chrome and Edge intensifies release frequency innovation.

title: Apple is reportedly skipping M6 Pro, Max, and Ultra chips to fast-track AI-focused M7 Macs
summary: Apple is reportedly skipping M6 Pro and Max variants to focus on M7 Macs for AI capabilities this fall, with M6 chips still expected for base Macs.
insight: Apple prioritizes AI-focused M7 Macs over high-end chip variants

title: SK Hynix predicts memory crunch will peak in 2027 and continue through 2030
summary: SK Hynix predicts a memory crunch peaking in 2027-2030 due to high demand.
insight: Memory supply struggles to keep pace with AI-driven demand surges.

title: Claude's new Reflect feature is Spotify Wrapped for your AI habits
summary: Claude's Reflect feature offers a personalized dashboard showcasing AI usage habits, including topics, activity patterns, and common tasks, all summarized over up to 12 months. This beta feature is accessible through the Claude desktop app on Windows or macOS. Users can gain valuable insights into their AI interactions.
insight: Unlocking AI habits for personal growth and optimization

title: New York hospital replaces 12 nurses with AI, prompting warnings over patient care dangers
summary: Montefiore Medical Center in the Bronx laid off 12 nurses, replacing them with AI-powered software. The AI software, provided by Datavant, is used for nonclinical tasks such as paperwork. The New York State Nurses Association expressed concerns over patient care.
insight: Replacing human nurses with AI may compromise patient care quality

title: PeaZip 11.2.0 brings quality-of-life improvements across the app
summary: PeaZip 11.2.0 introduces quality-of-life improvements, including a new function picker and smoother drag-and-drop. The update also expands support to 243 file extensions. This release enhances overall user experience.
insight: Enhanced user experience through improved functionality and support

title: TSMC posts blowout $39 billion quarter, raising the same old AI question: is this sustainable?
summary: TSMC reports a record $39 billion quarter, driven by strong chip demand. The company manufactures advanced chips for AI data centers, including those designed by Nvidia and Apple. As tech companies expand their data center capacity, demand for these chips remains robust.
insight: Robust chip demand fuels record quarter for TSMC's AI-focused manufacturing

title: ChatGPT Atlas lasted less than a year, but AI browser alternatives remain
summary: ChatGPT Atlas will shut down next month, less than a year after its launch. Mac users can still download it for a last look. Alternatives like Perplexity's Comet and Opera offer AI browser features.
insight: AI browser alternatives have a more sustainable future ahead.

title: Swedish startup unveils foot-long drone-killing interceptor that can exceed 220 mph
summary: Nordic Air Defence unveils the K100XR, a counter-drone system capable of exceeding 220 mph.
insight: Effective solution against rapidly advancing drone warfare threats emerges.

title: An abandoned IBM campus became a TikTok sensation, now it's a magnet for trespassers
summary: An abandoned IBM campus has become a popular destination for urban explorers, with footage of the site appearing on social media platforms like TikTok and Instagram.
insight: Abandoned corporate sites pose risks and challenges for law enforcement and property owners.

title: EU says it can't stop Sony from ending physical PlayStation game releases
summary: The EU says it cannot intervene in Sony's decision to end physical PlayStation game releases, opting for an all-digital future. European Commissioner Michael McGrath stated that the bloc lacks the power to control Sony's business strategies. This move may impact the used game market and consumer choices.
insight: EU regulatory powers are limited in controlling tech giants' business decisions
